title: Congenital Malignant Disorders
authors: Tiffany L. Lucas, Benjamin Huang & Elizabeth Robbins

blocks.text:
ทำไม neonatologist ต้องเข้าใจ Congenital Malignant Disorders?
Neonatal cancer พบน้อย (1:27,500 livebirths) แต่ presentation แตกต่างจาก older children อย่างมาก และ outcome ก็แตกต่าง. 6 สิ่งสำคัญ: (1) Neuroblastoma stage 4S = spontaneous regression ≥50% — ไม่ต้อง aggressive chemo; (2) ALL ใน neonates มี cure rate <50% ขณะที่เด็กโต >85% — aggressive biology, KMT2A rearrangement; (3) Sacrococcygeal teratoma = solid tumor ที่พบบ่อยที่สุดใน neonates; ต้อง excise early; (4) Skin nodule (bluish) + hepatomegaly ใน neonate = stage 4S neuroblastoma จนกว่าจะ rule out; (5) Down syndrome neonate → ตรวจ TMD ทุกราย (4–10% incidence), 20% develop ML-DS ใน 4 ปี; (6) AFP สูงในทารกแรกเกิดเป็น physiologic — ต้องใช้ age-specific reference values (Table 73.5).
เป้าหมายการเรียนรู้วันนี้
อธิบาย epidemiology ของ neonatal malignancy: incidence, distribution by type (Table 73.1), และ prognosis เทียบกับ older children
รู้ genetic predisposition syndromes สำคัญ (Table 73.3): BWS, Down, WAGR, Denys-Drash, Retinoblastoma (RB1), Li-Fraumeni, NF1
วินิจฉัยและจัดการ neuroblastoma ครบ: etiology, stage 4S/MS, MYCN amplification, catecholamine markers, INRG staging, treatment by stage
อธิบาย congenital leukemia: AML predominance, KMT2A/11q23, leukemia cutis, differential diagnosis, prognosis
รู้ Down syndrome: TMD (4–10%), ML-DS, GATA1 mutations, low-dose cytarabine indications
จำแนก germ cell tumors: sacrococcygeal teratoma (most common neonatal solid tumor), AFP interpretation, treatment
รู้ renal neoplasms: CMN (most common intrarenal), Wilms tumor, rhabdoid tumor (RTK) — prognosis แต่ละชนิด
อธิบาย retinoblastoma: RB1 genetics, leukocoria, bilateral = hereditary, treatment goals
รู้ infantile fibrosarcoma (cure rate ~100%), LCH, FHL/IAHS, hepatoblastoma ใน neonates
เข้าใจ treatment principles ใน neonates: chemo dosing (weight not BSA), radiation avoidance, pain, nutrition, blood products
แปลผล AFP age-specific values (Table 73.5) ใน neonates correctly
1. Epidemiology, Etiology, and Diagnosis
1.1 Incidence and Distribution
Incidence: 1 per 27,500 live births in US; 2% ของ childhood malignancies. Female = male incidence
Cancer ใน neonates พบน้อย แต่ mortality rates exceed those for older children even for identical diseases
1.2 Prognosis — Exceptions to Poor Neonatal Outcome
ALL: cure rate <50% in neonates vs >85% in older children — due to KMT2A rearrangement and aggressive biology
Neuroblastoma stage 4S: 5-year survival >90% in neonates with disseminated disease! Spontaneous regression in ≥50%
Infantile fibrosarcoma: cure rate approaches 100% — better in neonates than older children/adults
RMS, CNS tumors (PNET, ATRT, ependymoma): poorer survival in infants vs older children
1.3 Genetic Predisposition — Key Syndromes

Beckwith-Wiedemann syndrome (BWS): IGF2/H19 (11p15); macroglossia, gigantism, omphalocele, visceromegaly, neonatal hypoglycemia; 8% develop Wilms, hepatoblastoma, adrenal cortical carcinoma, RMS; abdominal US quarterly until age 8 yr
Down syndrome (Trisomy 21): 20× increased leukemia risk; TMD ใน 4–10% neonates; ML-DS ใน <3 yr
NF1 (NF1, 17q11.2): neurofibromin (GTPase-activating protein) → ลด Ras signaling; NF1 + JMML: LOH wild-type NF1 → ไม่มี 'off switch' → promote cell growth
WAGR syndrome (WT1+PAX6, 11p13): Wilms + aniridia + GU abnormalities + MR; ถ้าพบ aniridia → chromosome analysis → ถ้า 11p13 deletion → serial renal US
Retinoblastoma (RB1, 13q14): autosomal dominant, 90% penetrance; hereditary = bilateral/multifocal
Familial neuroblastoma (ALK, 2p23): 80% ของ familial cases; somatic ALK mutations ใน 12% sporadic tumors
PHOX2B mutation: Congenital central hypoventilation syndrome + Hirschsprung disease → increased risk neuroblastoma/ganglioneuroblastoma
1.4 Etiology — Non-Genetic Factors
Transplacental: extremely rare; leukemia, melanoma, lymphoma, hepatic carcinoma; frequency of maternal malignancy ≈1:1000 pregnancies but transplacental transmission rare (placenta protective)
Twin-to-twin: monozygotic twin: 25% chance developing leukemia (วันสัปดาห์-เดือนหลัง sibling) — via in utero transmission of preleukemic clone
Radiation: dose-response relationship; ≥10 milliGray → increased cancer risk; atomic bomb survivors data mixed
Maternal medications: DES → clear cell adenocarcinoma; alcohol → AML; topoisomerase 2 inhibitors (flavonoids, phytates) → AML; fertility drugs do NOT increase risk
1.5 Diagnosis and Evaluation
Symptoms ใน neonates มักไม่จำเพาะ: irritability, poor feeding, FTT, fever. ส่วนใหญ่ present เป็น mass; often identified prenatally by US; MRI postnatal to delineate
Labs: CBC, liver/renal function, urine catecholamines (VMA/HVA) ถ้า suspect neuroblastoma, serum AFP + β-hCG ถ้า suspect germ cell tumor, ferritin, LDH

AFP Age-Specific Normal Values (Table 73.5) — ห้ามใช้ adult reference!
Premature: mean 134,734 ng/mL. Newborn: mean 41,687. Day 1: 36,391. Day 7: 16,107. Days 22–28: mean 1,396. Days 61–90: mean 80. Days 181–720: mean 8. Half-life AFP = 5–7 days; β-hCG = 24–36 h. ค่าสูงใน neonate = physiologic; interpret using age-specific table.
2. Neuroblastoma — Most Common Neonatal Malignancy
2.1 Overview

Neuroblastoma เป็น malignant tumor ที่พบบ่อยที่สุดใน infancy; embryonal origin จาก neural crest cells (sympathoadrenal lineage). Neonatal neuroblastoma (age <28 days) = 5% ของทุก neuroblastoma cases; ≈100 cases/yr ใน North America diagnosed prenatally หรือ age <3 months.
2.2 Etiology and Associations
Genetic: Turner, Noonan (PTPN11 → leukemia+neuroblastoma), Costello syndrome; BWS: abdominal US quarterly until 8 yr
PHOX2B mutation + Hirschsprung → ganglioneuroblastoma risk; Familial: ALK germline mutation ใน 80%
2.3 Presentation ใน Neonates
Most common: asymptomatic adrenal mass on prenatal US (3rd trimester)
Stage 4S (MS): hepatomegaly (65%), subcutaneous metastases (32%); primary tumor เล็กหรือหาไม่พบ; bluish skin nodules — palpation causes transient erythema → blanching (catecholamine release)
Massive hepatomegaly → abdominal distention, coagulopathy, heart failure, respiratory distress → immediate chemotherapy
Horner syndrome: ptosis + miosis + enophthalmos + anhidrosis = stellate ganglion involvement
Spinal cord compression: paravertebral tumor → intervertebral foramina → paralysis; emergency chemo ± laminectomy ± radiation

Opsoclonus-myoclonus-ataxia (OMA — 'dancing eyes dancing feet'): autoimmune mechanism; antineuronal antibodies; treat with IVIG + steroids ± cyclophosphamide/rituximab; good survival but neurologic sequelae common
Catecholamines: >90% มี elevated urine VMA และ/หรือ HVA; measure before surgery/therapy; ใช้ monitor treatment response
2.4 Diagnosis
Imaging: US → CT/MRI primary; MIBG scan 90% sensitive (norepinephrine analogue → neuroblastoma bone+soft tissue); spine MRI ถ้า paraspinal/mediastinal
Bilateral bone marrow aspirate/biopsy (biopsy ใน >6 mo); tissue for histology + biologic studies
2.5 Pathology — Favorable vs Unfavorable (Shimada System)

Shimada 1984 / International Neuroblastoma Pathology Classification: (1) Neuroblastoma (undifferentiated/poorly differentiated/<5% diff / differentiating >5%); (2) Ganglioneuroblastoma intermixed; (3) Ganglioneuroblastoma nodular; (4) Ganglioneuroma
Classification based on: age + stroma amount + differentiation degree + mitosis-karyorrhexis index
2.6 Prognostic Factors — Tumor Biology (Table 73.6)
MYCN amplification (16–25%): poor prognosis ไม่ว่า stage. Stage 4 infants + MYCN amplified: progression-free survival <10% at 3 yr vs 93% single-copy
2.7 INRG Staging System (Table 73.7)
L1: localized, no vital structures involved, confined to 1 compartment (≈ INSS 1+2)
L2: locoregional + image-defined risk factors (≈ INSS 3)
M: distant metastatic (≈ INSS 4)
MS (formerly 4S): metastatic in infants <18 mo; mets confined to skin, liver, and/or bone marrow (<15% marrow); no skeletal mets on imaging; 5-yr survival >90%; spontaneous regression ≥50%
2.8 Treatment
Stage L1/L2: surgery alone; 96–100% survival
Isolated adrenal mass (prenatal), no symptoms: monitor for spontaneous regression ถ้า tumor meets size criteria + VMA/HVA decreasing

Stage MS (4S): highly favorable — minimal or no therapy; supportive care; chemo หรือ surgery เฉพาะ symptomatic relief. Main cause of death = massive hepatomegaly → respiratory failure/organ compromise. Low-dose radiation 450 cGy (3 fractions) ถ้า chemo ไม่ response
Stage 3+4: chemo + surgery ± radiation; active drugs: cisplatin/carboplatin, etoposide, doxorubicin, cyclophosphamide, vincristine
High-risk (MYCN amplified, Stage 4): intensive chemo → myeloablative therapy + stem cell support + isotretinoin + anti-GD2 antibody (ch14.18/dinutuximab) (Yu NEJM 2010)
3. Congenital Leukemia
3.1 Epidemiology

Congenital leukemia = diagnosed ใน 4 wk แรก; ≈5 cases per million ใน 3 months แรก. 2/3 เป็น AML (contrast: older children = ALL predominant). Overall survival at 24 months: only 20% due to aggressive biology + age-related treatment complications
Most, if not all, cases initiated in utero; KMT2A (11q23) rearrangements found in archived newborn blood spots before clinical leukemia (retrospective studies)
3.2 Clinical Manifestations
Hepatosplenomegaly, petechiae, ecchymoses — อาจพบตั้งแต่แรกเกิด
Leukemia cutis (25–30%): myeloid cutaneous infiltration; multiple movable nodules, greenish-blue or dark pink; may be FIRST sign of leukemia; biopsy + flow cytometry + cytogenetics on skin biopsy
Respiratory distress: hyperleukocytosis → blast infiltration lungs; CNS leukostasis → somnolence, apnea
Auer rods: intracellular inclusions pathognomonic of AML (Fig 73.5)
WBC: leukocytosis common; WBC 150,000–250,000/mm³ or higher (up to 1,300,000/mm³ reported); blast predominance on differential
3.3 Differential Diagnosis
Congenital infections (CMV, herpes, toxoplasma, syphilis): similar hepatosplenomegaly + pancytopenia
Severe erythroblastosis fetalis: nucleated RBCs, extramedullary erythropoiesis skin infiltrates (rare) mimicking leukemia cutis
TMD in Down syndrome: clinically indistinguishable from AML — see below
Neuroblastoma: hepatomegaly + bluish skin nodules similar; CBC มักปกติ ไม่มี circulating blasts; bone marrow อาจมี neuroblast clusters; catecholamines (VMA/HVA) ↑ → neuroblastoma
3.4 Genetics — KMT2A (MLL) at 11q23

KMT2A rearrangements: 50% ของ infant AML, 70–80% ของ infant ALL; nonhereditary, nonconstitutional; confer poor prognosis, worsening with decreasing age; KMT2A positively regulates HOX genes → hematopoietic development
3.5 Treatment and Prognosis
AML: daunorubicin + cytarabine; overall survival 65–75% (similar to older children). CBFA2T3-GLIS2 fusion (rare form) = poor survival
ALL: disease-free survival 28–47% (Pieters Lancet 2007, Interfant-99); improved with hybrid ALL+AML regimens addressing KMT2A-rearranged ALL. Congenital ALL: <20% disease-free survival
Intrathecal chemo important (CNS involvement common); radiation avoided (neurocognitive sequelae + secondary malignancies)
3.6 Transient Myeloproliferative Disorder (TMD) — Down Syndrome
TMD ใน 4–10% ของ Down syndrome neonates; clinically indistinguishable from AML; GATA1 somatic mutations ใน nearly all TMD + ML-DS cases
Most resolve spontaneously within 3 months. 20% ของ Down TMD cases fatal (hepatic or cardiopulmonary failure); ~20% develop ML-DS within 4 yr → preleukemic syndrome
Treatment indications (low-dose cytarabine): WBC >50,000–100,000/mm³; plt <100,000; hepatic/renal dysfunction; cardiopulmonary compromise
ML-DS (myeloid leukemia associated with Down syndrome): 20-fold higher AML risk vs general population; rare megakaryoblastic subtype; favorable prognosis — hypersensitive to cytarabine/daunorubicin
4. Germ Cell Tumors
Germ cell tumors = 35–40% ของทุก neoplasms ใน neonate แต่มีเพียง 5% เท่านั้นที่มี malignant component. Sacrococcygeal = most common site (40% of all GCT); followed by neck.
4.1 Sacrococcygeal Teratoma
Most common solid tumor in newborns; incidence 1 in 27,000 livebirths; females 2–4× more common; 50% diagnosed prenatally by US
Malignant component: 10–17% contain yolk sac tumor; 7–10% in infants <2 months. Yolk sac tumor = most common malignant GCT in neonates

Pathology: all 3 embryonic layers present; ectodermal (glial tissue, skin/hair/teeth), mesodermal (fat/bone/muscle), endodermal (GI tissue); AFP ↑ = endodermal sinus elements; β-hCG ↑ = embryonal carcinoma/choriocarcinoma
Prenatal complications: polyhydramnios, non-immune hydrops, dystocia; lack of prenatal diagnosis → 20% mortality ใน 1 h แรกหลังเกิด (hemorrhage)
Treatment: radical surgical excision ASAP + remove coccyx; failure to remove coccyx = 30–40% local recurrence; survival 85% overall; prenatally-diagnosed = only 30–50% (hydrops + prematurity)
ถ้ามี malignant yolk sac elements: surgery + chemo (cisplatin, etoposide, bleomycin); late effects: hearing loss, pulmonary fibrosis, secondary malignancy
5. Renal Neoplasms
5.1 Congenital Mesoblastic Nephroma (CMN)
Most common intrarenal neoplasm in neonates; mostly benign; cellular variant occasionally recurs/metastasizes
Presentation: asymptomatic abdominal mass; hypertension (↑ renin); hypercalcemia
Two subtypes: (1) Classic (mean age 1 month); (2) Cellular variant (mean >3 months): t(12;15)(p13;q25) → ETV6-NTRK3 fusion = same as infantile fibrosarcoma
Treatment: complete surgical resection; survival 95–98%
5.2 Wilms Tumor (Nephroblastoma)
Most common intra-abdominal tumor in childhood (1:8000 children); rare in neonatal period; arises from abnormal proliferation of metanephric cells (nephrogenic rests)
Syndromes: WAGR (WT1+PAX6 deletion, 11p13), Denys-Drash (WT1 missense), BWS (11p15), hemihypertrophy, Frasier syndrome, Perlman, Trisomy 18
Prognostic factors: anaplasia (diffuse worse than focal); LOH 1p+16q = worse prognosis
Treatment: low-stage = surgery alone (>90% cure); intermediate = vincristine + dactinomycin 12 weeks; advanced/anaplasia = + doxorubicin ± radiation; metastatic disease = 70% long-term survival
5.3 Rhabdoid Tumor of the Kidney (RTK)
Second most common malignant renal neoplasm in neonates after Wilms; mortality >80%; most lethal neoplasm of early neonatal life
INI1 gene (SMARCB1, 22q11.2) — homozygous inactivating deletions; detected by immunohistochemistry. Survival at 2 yr for infants <6 months: <15%; virtually all distant metastases = fatal
6. Retinoblastoma
Incidence ≈ 1:18,000 livebirths; 250–350 cases/yr US; bilateral in 20–35% — all bilateral = hereditary

RB1 (13q14): tumor suppressor; hereditary = germline mutation (autosomal dominant, 90% penetrance) — often bilateral, mean diagnosis 1 yr; sporadic = somatic mutations both alleles — unilateral, mean 2 yr
5% ของ retinoblastoma patients มี constitutional 13q deletion (13q−): associated anomalies — microencephaly, macrognathia, ptosis, short stature
Presentation: leukocoria ('cat's eye'), squinting, strabismus; multifocal retinal involvement 84%; extraocular spread <15% (via optic nerve → subarachnoid → intracranial)
Trilateral retinoblastoma: bilateral retinoblastoma + pineal gland tumor; brain MRI annual until age 5–6 yr
Diagnosis: fundoscopy under anesthesia + orbital US; MRI orbit (extent + optic nerve); LP ถ้า optic nerve invasion

Treatment goal: local control + preserve vision; enucleation reserved เฉพาะ no chance of useful vision or failed conservative. Small tumors: cryotherapy + laser photocoagulation + systemic chemo (carboplatin/vincristine/etoposide) ± intra-arterial melphalan
Prognosis: unilateral 85–90% cure; bilateral: lower due to high risk second malignancies throughout life (radiation-induced sarcomas in irradiated AND non-irradiated areas in hereditary disease)
7. Other Neonatal Malignancies
7.1 Infantile/Congenital Fibrosarcoma
Most common soft tissue sarcoma in neonatal age group; low-grade non-RMS; cure rate approaches 100% (much better than older children/adults)
Cytogenetics: t(12;15)(p13;q25) = ETV6-NTRK3 fusion (same as cellular CMN) → target for TRK inhibitors
Treatment: complete surgical excision; neoadjuvant chemo for tumor shrinkage (vincristine/dactinomycin/cyclophosphamide, or etoposide/ifosfamide); radiation avoided
7.2 Langerhans Cell Histiocytosis (LCH)
Most common histiocytic disease in fetus/neonate; ≈50% confined to skin; other half = disseminated (bone marrow, liver, lung)
Diagnosis: biopsy + immunohistochemistry CD1a and S-100 positive

Pure cutaneous: spontaneous resolution 2–3 months; disseminated = 57% mortality; treat with vinblastine + prednisone. BRAF V600E mutation (recent): vemurafenib effective in refractory cases (Donadieu JCO 2019)
7.3 Hemophagocytic Lymphohistiocytosis (HLH) — Familial (FHL) and IAHS
FHL incidence ≈1:50,000; overall survival without stem cell transplant = only 9%; with SCT = 64%

Diagnostic criteria (HLH-2004 — Box 73.1): ≥5 of 8 criteria: fever, splenomegaly, cytopenias, hypertriglyceridemia/hypofibrinogenemia, hemophagocytosis in BM, ↓ NK cell activity, ferritin ≥500, elevated soluble CD25 (≥2400 IU/mL)
FHL genetics: PRF1 (30%), UNC13D, STX11 mutations; whole exome sequencing ถ้าไม่มี known genetic predisposition
Treatment: steroids + etoposide → SCT ถ้า progressive
IAHS (infection-associated): similar presentation; survival 59% with appropriate antibiotic/antiviral treatment
7.4 Hepatoblastoma
Most common malignant liver tumor in children; incidence 11.2/million infants; premature/low birthweight infants มี high incidence; female:male = 1:6 in neonates
Associated: BWS, trisomy 18, familial adenomatous polyposis (APC germline mutation ใน 10% of sporadic hepatoblastoma)
AFP elevated above neonatal norms ใน half of patients; US → CT/MRI → PRETEXT staging system → guide resection vs transplant
Pure fetal histology + complete resection: 92% 24-month survival; overall 86%. Unresectable non-metastatic: cisplatin + vincristine → surgical resection; orthotopic liver transplant ถ้า unresectable.
8. Treatment Considerations in Neonates
8.1 Chemotherapy Dosing
Dose ตาม weight ไม่ใช่ BSA (ลด overall dose); increased total body water, ↓ cytochrome P450, ↓ renal function ใน neonates
Intrathecal chemo: adjusted for age — CSF volume relative to BSA ใหญ่กว่าผู้ใหญ่มาก → avoid underdosing ใน infant leukemia
Vesicants (vincristine, doxorubicin): ต้องมี central venous catheter; skin burns ถ้า extravasated peripheral
8.2 ข้อควรระวังพิเศษ

⚠ Rasburicase CONTRAINDICATED ใน G6PD Deficiency
Rasburicase (recombinant urate oxidase) ใช้ใน tumor lysis syndrome → CONTRAINDICATED ถ้า G6PD deficiency — รายงาน severe hemolysis และ death ใน preterm neonate (Zaramella Pediatrics 2013). ตรวจ G6PD ก่อนใช้ rasburicase ในทุก high-risk ethnicity
Blood products: CMV-negative, irradiated, leukocyte-depleted; donor-designated blood discouraged ใน congenital leukemia (possible future related-donor SCT)
Radiation: avoid ถ้าเป็นไปได้ — late effects: cognitive impairment, growth failure, secondary malignancies. Reserve for acute life-threatening situations
Immunizations: avoid until ≥6 months ไม่ได้รับ chemo; close contacts ให้ inactivated (not live) polio vaccine
9. Bedside Pearls

PEARL 1 — Bluish Skin Nodule + Hepatomegaly = Neuroblastoma Stage 4S
Bluish subcutaneous nodule ใน neonate ที่มี hepatomegaly → neuroblastoma stage 4S จนกว่าจะ rule out. Check urine VMA/HVA, abdominal US. อย่าลืม: 50% cases spontaneous regression โดยไม่ต้องรักษา.

PEARL 2 — Down Syndrome Neonate: ตรวจ CBC ทุกราย
TMD เกิดใน 4–10% ของ Down syndrome neonates; clinically = AML; resolves most. แต่ 20% fatal (hepatic/cardiopulmonary) และ 20% → ML-DS ภายใน 4 ปี. CBC + peripheral smear ทุก Down syndrome newborn; low-dose cytarabine ถ้า WBC >50–100K, plt <100K, organ dysfunction.

PEARL 3 — AFP ใน Neonate ≠ Cancer Without Age-Specific Reference
Newborn AFP mean = 41,687 ng/mL; premature mean = 134,734 ng/mL. AFP ปกติสำหรับ neonate จะสูงมากเมื่อเทียบกับ adult reference. ใช้ Table 73.5 เสมอ; half-life AFP = 5–7 days — ถ้าไม่ลดตาม expected → suspect residual/recurrent malignancy.

PEARL 4 — Sacrococcygeal Teratoma: ต้อง Excise Coccyx ด้วย
Failure to remove entire coccyx → 30–40% local recurrence, sometimes with malignant transformation. Prenatal diagnosis (50% of cases) = worse prognosis (30–50% survival) due to hydrops + prematurity. 10–17% contain malignant yolk sac tumor.

PEARL 5 — Infantile Fibrosarcoma: Cure Rate ~100% ใน Neonates
ETV6-NTRK3 fusion = targetable (TRK inhibitors); same translocation เป็น cellular CMN. Cure rates approach 100% ใน neonates — ไม่ต้องใช้ radical surgery ก็ได้; neoadjuvant chemo ลด tumor size ก่อน. Doxorubicin avoided (cardiac toxicity).
10. Evidence Update — Landmark Studies
Mitchell Clin Perinatol 2021: comprehensive review of cancer predisposition syndromes in neonates/infants — recognition, tumor types, surveillance
Linabery & Ross Cancer 2008: trends in childhood cancer incidence US 1992–2004; neuroblastoma 54/million infants = most common
Twist JCO 2019 (COG ANBL0531): risk factors for chemo in stage 4S neuroblastoma — defined when treatment required; low-dose radiation 450 cGy in 3 fractions for resistant hepatic disease
Yu NEJM 2010: anti-GD2 antibody ch14.18 + GM-CSF + IL-2 + isotretinoin → improved event-free survival in high-risk neuroblastoma
van der Linden Blood 2009 (Interfant-99): congenital ALL treated with Interfant-99 protocol → disease-free survival <20% in <28 d, 28–47% in 1–12 mo
Pieters Lancet 2007 (Interfant-99): treatment protocol for ALL <1 yr; randomized 4th element adding AML-based therapy
Gamis Blood 2011 (COG A2971): natural history of TMD in Down syndrome neonates — 20% develop ML-DS within 4 yr; administration of low-dose cytarabine does not alter ML-DS development
Creutzig Leukemia 2012 (AML-BFM): favorable outcome ใน infant AML after intensive treatment; overall survival 65–75%
Zaramella Pediatrics 2013: lethal effect of single-dose rasburicase in preterm neonate with G6PD deficiency — avoid in high-risk patients
Donadieu JCO 2019: vemurafenib (BRAF inhibitor) effective in refractory multisystem LCH in children — BRAF V600E mutation targetable
Glick JPedSurg 2004: renal tumors in infants <6 mo — CMN most common, ETV6-NTRK3 fusion in cellular variant; surgical resection curative in most
11. Self-Check Q&A
Q: Neonate Day 3 มี hepatomegaly + 3–4 bluish skin nodules ที่ back, CBC ปกติ — diagnosis ที่ต้องนึกถึงอันดับแรก?

ตอบ: Neuroblastoma stage 4S (MS). Bluish nodules + hepatomegaly ใน neonate = classic 4S presentation. Palpation → transient erythema → blanching (catecholamine release). Check urine VMA/HVA, abdominal US, serum ferritin, LDH. ตาม INRG staging: MS = metastatic in <18 mo confined to skin/liver/bone marrow, MYCN nonamplified → excellent prognosis, spontaneous regression >50%.
Q: Down syndrome neonate Day 1, WBC 85,000/mm³ พร้อม 30% blasts, hepatomegaly — ควรทำอะไร?

ตอบ: วินิจฉัย: Transient Myeloproliferative Disorder (TMD). GATA1 somatic mutation in nearly all cases. WBC 85,000 + hepatomegaly = indication for treatment (threshold WBC >50–100K). Low-dose cytarabine ลด risk early death. ตาม follow-up: 20% fatal (hepatic/cardiopulmonary), 20% → ML-DS ใน 4 ปี → close hematology follow-up mandatory.
Q: Neonate มี sacrococcygeal mass 5×7 cm พบใน prenatal US — preoperative workup?

ตอบ: AFP (age-specific reference) + β-hCG; MRI sacrococcygeal region (spinal cord involvement?, rectal wall?); chest CT (metastasis?); abdominal MRI (local invasion). Surgery: radical excision ASAP + remove entire coccyx. ถ้า contain malignant yolk sac elements: cisplatin/etoposide/bleomycin. Prenatal diagnosis = 30–50% survival (vs 85% postnatal) due to hydrops risk.
Q: Infant 28 days, white pupillary reflex (leukocoria) ตาขวา — diagnosis? Investigation?

ตอบ: Retinoblastoma. Presentation: leukocoria ('cat's eye'), squinting, strabismus. ตรวจ: fundoscopy under GA + orbital US; MRI orbit (optic nerve involvement); brain MRI (trilateral retinoblastoma — pineal gland). Family history → genetic counselling + RB1 testing. ถ้า bilateral = hereditary; screen ตาอีกข้างและ annual brain MRI until age 5–6 yr.
Q: Infant 1 wk มี abdominal mass, HTN, US พบ right renal mass not encapsulated infiltrating renal parenchyma — most likely diagnosis?

ตอบ: Congenital mesoblastic nephroma (CMN) = most common intrarenal neoplasm in neonates. ไม่ encapsulated, infiltrates renal parenchyma = characteristic. HTN จาก mass effect on renal vasculature. Classic subtype (mean 1 month). Complete surgical resection = curative; survival 95–98%. Cellular variant (ETV6-NTRK3 fusion) มี recurrence risk → ติดตามใกล้ชิด.
Q: คุณแม่ประวัติ aniridia ตาขวา + ลูกชายแรกเกิดปกติ — surveillance ที่แนะนำ?

ตอบ: Aniridia ใน mother = PAX6 deletion; แต่ถ้า WT1 deletion (11p13) ด้วย = WAGR syndrome → ลูกเสี่ยง Wilms tumor. Chromosome analysis on infant. ถ้า chromosome 11p13 deletion → serial renal ultrasound surveillance สม่ำเสมอ; Wilms develops in ~50% of WAGR patients. BWS (11p15) → abdominal US quarterly until age 8 yr.
Q: Neonate มี WBC 220,000/mm³ + Auer rods ใน blast cells + leukemia cutis — ขั้นตอนถัดไป?

ตอบ: AML (Auer rods pathognomonic) + leukemia cutis. Skin biopsy: flow cytometry + cytogenetics (KMT2A/11q23 rearrangement?) — KMT2A rearrangement found ใน 50% infant AML. Supportive care: correct hyperleukocytosis (ระวัง TLS), DIC (FFP + platelets). Chemo: daunorubicin + cytarabine. Avoid rasburicase ถ้า G6PD deficiency; use irradiated CMV-negative blood products.

Day 74 Preview — Renal Development (Avery Ch.74)
พรุ่งนี้ครอบคลุม renal development ตั้งแต่ pronephros → mesonephros → metanephros และ nephrogenesis สมบูรณ์เมื่อ 36 wk. จะเชื่อมกับ Day 73 ตรงที่ Wilms tumor เกิดจาก nephrogenic rests — residual metanephric tissue ที่ยังไม่ differentiates normally; และ BWS/WAGR syndromes เริ่มจาก 11p ซึ่งเกี่ยวกับ renal developmental genes. Renal agenesis, dysplasia, polycystic kidneys จะ cover ใน Ch.75.
